field_requirements.rationale: SOC 2 requires logical and physical controls to protect confidential information including encryption key management
SOC 2 requires comprehensive audit trails capturing all system access and data modifications
SEC Rule 17a-4 under SOX Section 802 requires minimum 7-year retention (2555 days) of audit records related to financial reporting and corporate fraud investigations
SOC 2 requires access to system resources be restricted to authorized personnel within organizational boundaries
Privileged access management supports SOC 2 requirements for access review, least privilege, and segregation of duties
SCC Premium provides continuous security monitoring and vulnerability detection aligned with SOC 2 monitoring requirements
Event Threat Detection enables real-time identification and alerting on security anomalies per SOC 2 incident response criteria
SOC 2 requires centralized collection and correlation of security events across all system components
SEC Rule 17a-4 under SOX Section 802 mandates 7-year retention (2555 days) of records relevant to financial reporting, requiring long-term immutable log storage
No notification channels are configured. Security alerts and incident notifications have nowhere to go. At least one channel must be configured for SOC 2 monitoring requirements.
SOC 2 Availability criteria requires disaster recovery and business continuity planning to ensure system availability commitments
Cloud Run services should restrict ingress to internal traffic and GCLB to support logical access controls and boundary protection
Cloud Functions should restrict ingress to internal traffic and GCLB to support logical access controls and boundary protection
Cloud Storage containing federal data must use CMEK with FIPS 140-2 validated encryption
BigQuery datasets containing federal data must use CMEK with FIPS 140-2 validated encryption
Cloud SQL databases containing federal data must use CMEK with FIPS 140-2 validated encryption
FedRAMP requires visibility into cloud provider access to federal data for accountability and audit purposes
FedRAMP requires categorization and classification of federal information to apply appropriate security controls
FedRAMP requires federal data to remain within US boundaries; resource location enforcement prevents deployment to non-US regions
FedRAMP requires federal data sovereignty within US boundaries; only US locations should be permitted
Assured Workloads provides Google-managed compliance controls required for FedRAMP authorization boundary, including data residency enforcement, support access restrictions, and encryption guarantees
Access Approval requires explicit customer consent before Google support personnel can access federal data, required for FedRAMP High and recommended for Moderate
Access Approval paired with Access Transparency ensures full audit trail and customer control over Google admin access to federal resources
GKE application-layer secrets encryption ensures Kubernetes secrets are encrypted with CMEK at rest, required when GKE handles federal data
Firestore databases containing federal data must use CMEK to meet FIPS 140-2 encryption requirements
FedRAMP requires strict control over service account credentials to prevent unauthorized access to federal systems
Federal resources must access Google APIs through private network paths to enforce boundary protection and prevent public exposure
DNSSEC provides authentication of DNS responses, preventing DNS spoofing and cache poisoning attacks against federal information systems
VPC Service Controls enforce network boundary protection and prevent data exfiltration from federal information system perimeters
Binary Authorization enforces deployment of only trusted and verified container images, supporting configuration management and software integrity controls
VPC Service Controls in dry-run mode do not enforce perimeter protection. For production federal workloads, enforcement mode is required to prevent data exfiltration
ALWAYS_ALLOW mode defeats the purpose of Binary Authorization. Production deployments should use REQUIRE_ATTESTATION to ensure only verified container images are deployed
FedRAMP requires regular testing of contingency plans including failover and recovery procedures
GKE cluster secrets must be encrypted with application-layer encryption when processing federal data
BigQuery datasets containing federal data must use CMEK with FIPS 140-2 validated encryption
Cloud Storage buckets containing federal data must use CMEK with FIPS 140-2 validated encryption
Cloud SQL databases containing federal data must use CMEK with FIPS 140-2 validated encryption
Firestore databases containing federal data must use CMEK with FIPS 140-2 validated encryption
Pub/Sub topics carrying federal data messages must use CMEK with FIPS 140-2 validated encryption
Dataflow jobs processing federal data must use CMEK with FIPS 140-2 validated encryption
Secure Boot verifies that boot firmware and OS are signed by trusted authorities, protecting against boot-level malware in federal compute environments

org_policy_requirements.rationale: Prevents unmanaged service account keys that undermine access control governance
Shielded VMs protect compute instances from boot-level and firmware attacks
Serial port access represents an unaudited access path that bypasses IAM controls
Restricts resource creation to approved geographic locations for federal data sovereignty compliance
Restricts IAM policy members to approved organizational domains, preventing unauthorized external access to federal resources

values.landing_zone_description: acme landing zone for USA government agency v4

vpc_networks.description: Hub network for transit and connectivity
vpc_networks.name: vpc-hub
